articles.title: LAUNCH WEEK
articles.summary: Revenue shouldn't fracture just because your go-to-market motions do. Most billing systems force companies to juggle separate tools for self-serve, enterprise, and cloud marketplaces—creating data silos, reconciliation gaps, and a mess for customers and finance teams alike.

articles.title: 👉 Read more on the Metronome blog
AI EATS THE WORLD
articles.summary: AI is reshaping tech. Defensibility now comes from workflow ownership, proprietary data, distribution, and seamless UX. Superintelligence is on the horizon. Startups must 10x workflows, keep humans involved where needed, and anchor themselves in the layer of the stack that will keep long-term value.

articles.title: PRODUCT DISCOVERY MODELS
articles.summary: There are four ways companies approach product discovery, depending on how centralized and how evidence-driven they are. The strongest results come from empowered teams using data, but most organizations drift toward top-down control unless they deliberately choose otherwise.

articles.title: WHY STRATEGIC THINKING MATTERS MORE THAN THE STRATEGY
articles.summary: Do not wait for a perfect strategy. Use the clues you already have about goals and customers to make explicit choices. Write down your assumptions, and move the work forward.

articles.title: SOME MISSING AI MONEY STORIES
articles.summary: Companies are overspending on AI out of fear and hype, but this phase will not last. Product leaders must be ready with clear financial cases for where AI can deliver meaningful returns.

articles.title: of both. See what you can build with Marqeta
HOW TO STAND OUT DURING PRODUCT INTERVIEWS IN THE ERA OF AI
articles.summary: Getting a PM job today means showing curiosity about AI, doing deep product research, and building quick prototypes before the interview. The candidates who actively demonstrate value, guide the conversation, and follow up with insight are the ones who stand out.

articles.title: THE SIMPLE SECRET TO INFLUENCE
articles.summary: True leadership is not about showing you are smart, but helping others feel smart. Leaders who ask questions, listen well, and give credit build deeper trust and far greater influence.

articles.title: GAS-FIRED INTELLIGENCE
articles.summary: US natural gas is no longer an endless, cheap oversupply. LNG exports and AI data centers are competing for the same molecules. In the coming years, gas is likely to become the core near-term power source for AI. Some export ambitions will be curtailed so domestic demand can be met.

articles.title: RECOGNIZING HOW BIAS COMPOUNDS AT LEADERSHIP LEVELS
articles.summary: Affinity bias, confirmation bias, and negativity bias quietly limit opportunities for women and minorities. Small, often hidden decisions accumulate over time, shaping who advances and who gets left behind.

articles.summary: High agency comes from testing ideas early, learning from evidence, and reducing the distance between belief and truth.
